cards.front: What are the two most common causes of acute pancreatitis?

cards.back: Periumbilical bruising — indicates severe hemorrhagic pancreatitis
Flank bruising — indicates retroperitoneal hemorrhage in severe pancreatitis

cards.front: Which enzyme is more specific for pancreatitis: amylase or lipase?
What are Ranson's criteria at admission? (GA LAW)
What Ranson score indicates severe pancreatitis?
When should CT be done in acute pancreatitis?
cards.back: At 72 hours (to assess necrosis) — not on admission

cards.front: What is the role of antibiotics in acute pancreatitis?
cards.back: Not routine; only for infected necrosis

cards.front: When should feeding be started in acute pancreatitis?
cards.back: Early (24-48 hours) — enteral preferred over parenteral

cards.front: What is the step-up approach for infected necrosis?
What ALT level suggests gallstone etiology?

cards.back: Peripancreatic fluid collection with defined wall, occurring >4 weeks after acute pancreatitis

cards.front: What CRP level at 48 hours suggests severe pancreatitis?
What is the GET SMASHED mnemonic?
cards.back: Gallstones, Ethanol, Trauma, Steroids, Mumps/Malignancy, Autoimmune, Scorpion, Hyperlipidemia/Hypercalcemia, ERCP, Drugs

cards.front: When is ERCP indicated in gallstone pancreatitis?
cards.back: If cholangitis present or CBD stone confirmed
